parameters.query:
SELECT
  i.id AS item_id,
  i.source_id,
  i.title,
  i.url,
  i.published_at,
  i.raw_content
FROM items i
LEFT JOIN item_enriched ie ON ie.item_id = i.id
WHERE ie.item_id IS NULL
  AND i.published_at::date >= $1
  AND i.published_at::date <= $2
ORDER BY i.id;

INSERT INTO item_enriched (
  item_id,
  language,
  category,
  tags,
  short_summary,
  detailed_summary,
  importance_score,
  virality_score
)
VALUES (
  (($1::json)->>'item_id')::integer,
  ($1::json)->>'language',
  ($1::json)->>'category',
  (($1::json)->'tags')::jsonb,
  ($1::json)->>'short_summary',
  ($1::json)->>'detailed_summary',
  NULLIF((($1::json)->>'importance_score')::text, '')::numeric,
  NULLIF((($1::json)->>'virality_score')::text, '')::numeric
)
ON CONFLICT (item_id) DO NOTHING;

values.content:
=TASK: Analyze {{ $json.items.length }} articles for a {{ $json.topic }} digest (Batch {{ $json.batch_info.batch_number }}/{{ $json.batch_info.total_batches }}).

ARTICLES:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.items, null, 2) }}

REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT - CRITICAL:
Return ONLY a JSON array with {{ $json.items.length }} objects, one per article, in exact order.

EXACT STRUCTURE (no variations):
[
  {
    "language": "en",
    "category": "category_name",
    "tags": ["tag1", "tag2", "tag3"],
    "short_summary": "2-3 sentence summary for non-technical readers.",
    "detailed_summary": "1-3 paragraphs with context and implications.",
    "importance_score": 0.85,
    "virality_score": 0.70
  }
]

STRICT RULES:
- NO wrapper objects (no "digest", no "articles", just the array)
- NO markdown blocks or code fences
- NO contractions (use: it is, do not, cannot)
- NO special quotes or dashes (use plain - not — or –)
- Start with [ and end with ]
- Return {{ $json.items.length }} complete objects

BEGIN OUTPUT NOW:

parameters.query:
SELECT 
  value->>'current_topic' as topic,
  value->>'period_start' as period_start,
  value->>'period_end' as period_end
FROM system_state
WHERE key = 'current_cycle';
